Title: Plastid-localized ZmENR1/ZmHAD1 complex ensures maize pollen and anther development through regulating lipid and ROS metabolism

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: β-hydroxyacyl-ACP dehydratase; type II fatty acid synthase component
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: anther cuticle lipids and pollen exine precursors
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: anther epidermal cuticle and pollen exine
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes maize male fertility and supports anther cuticle/pollen exine development by enhancing ZmENR1-dependent fatty acid biosynthesis; knockout causes partial male sterility with aborted pollen.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmHAD1 physically interacts with plastid-localized ZmENR1 and forms a heterodimer. 2. Co-incubation with ZmHAD1 increases ZmENR1 catalytic efficiency in vitro. 3. CRISPR knockout of ZmHAD1 causes partial male sterility (~18% aborted pollen), indicating a positive but partially redundant role in male reproduction.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS; Y2H; LCI; BiFC; Co-IP; in vitro enzyme activity assay; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; pollen phenotype analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression overlaps with ZmENR1 from anther stages S6-S10
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: IP-MS: PRIDE PXD055301; ChIP-seq dataset used in study: GSE220176; lipid metabolomics: Metabolomics Workbench PR002201
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes partial male sterility; possible functional redundancy with ZmHAD2 discussed.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a common/core partner rather than the primary focal catalytic determinant for the terminal metabolite; transcript level is unchanged in enr1 anthers, suggesting enr1 sterility is not due to altered ZmHAD1 expression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential partner target for improving male fertility stability or engineering genic male sterility, but redundancy should be considered.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: anther cuticle lipids and pollen exine precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: anther epidermal cuticle and pollen exine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for male fertility by enabling anther cuticle and pollen exine formation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastid type II fatty acid synthase pathway in anther tapetum
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream reductive step of each fatty acid elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces trans-2-enoyl-ACP analog crotonyl-CoA to butyryl-CoA in an NADH-dependent reaction, completing the final reduction step of the elongation cycle in de novo fatty acid synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for cutin/wax and sporopollenin precursor biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for anther-specific de novo fatty acid biosynthesis; essential for male fertility in maize
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes de novo fatty acid biosynthesis, anther cuticle and pollen exine formation, and proper ROS homeostasis; loss causes complete male sterility, early tapetal PCD, defective cuticle, thinner exine, and no pollen.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EMS mapping, CRISPR knockout, complementation, and allelism tests identified ZmENR1 as the causal gene for the enr1 male-sterile mutant. 2. ZmENR1 is tapetum-specific, plastid-localized, and highly expressed at anther stages S7-S9 with peak at S9. 3. Loss of ZmENR1 triggers premature tapetal PCD, excessive ROS/H2O2, increased malate and mitochondrial Complex I activity, and downregulates sporopollenin/cutin-wax genes. 4. ZmENR1 directly supports lipid precursor supply for anther cuticle and pollen exine formation. 5. ZmHAD1 interaction enhances ZmENR1 catalytic activity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Tapetum-specific; highly expressed in anthers from S7 to S9, peaking at S9; protein accumulates mainly in tapetum at S9-S10
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tapetum-specific in developing anthers at S7-S10
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural growth conditions; developmental stage-dependent effects during anther stages S7-S13
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; EMS mutant analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; genetic complementation; allelic test; qPCR; western blot; immunohistochemistry; GFP localization; enzyme assay; cytology; GC-MS lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Anther RNA-seq referenced for candidate selection; IP-MS: PRIDE PXD055301; lipid metabolomics: Metabolomics Workbench PR002201; ChIP-seq dataset used in study: GSE220176
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss blocks male fertility; combined mutation with paralogs leads severe vegetative defects/lethality; perturbation affects ROS metabolism and multiple lipid classes.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native ZmENR1 promoter in complementation construct (pZmENR1-GFP-ZmENR1); constitutive promoters used for transient assays/CRISPR are not central to phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Five residues T210, S222, Y248, Y258, and K266 are essential for activity. ZmENR1 likely promotes synthesis of C16:0, C18:1, and C16:0 alcohol precursors feeding ER lipid pathways. Homologous ENR1 genes in rice and Arabidopsis also regulate fertility/development.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for genic male sterility systems in maize; useful for hybrid breeding and for engineering tapetum-specific lipid flux, but editing must avoid compensation/lethality from broader ENR family disruption.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: anther/pollen lipid structures required for fertility
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastid type II fatty acid synthase pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: same ENR reduction step in elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ENR ortholog inferred to catalyze the final reduction step of plastid fatty acid elongation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies fatty acid precursors required for male reproductive development
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential reproductive ENR paralog in rice
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for male fertility; CRISPR mutants show complete male sterility with smaller anthers and no visible pollen grains, while vegetative growth remains normal.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsENR1 is highly expressed in many tissues, especially anthers. 2. CRISPR knockout lines exhibit complete male sterility with smaller anthers and no visible pollen. 3. Together with OsENR2, it redundantly supports vegetative viability, as double mutants show seedling lethality-like defects.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in all detected tissues, especially anther
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; reproductive phenotype analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single knockout causes male sterility; double knockout with OsENR2 causes severe vegetative defects and death.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because ortholog function was directly tested in the Results section, showing conserved ENR1 function in flowering plants.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for rice genic male sterility and hybrid seed production.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fatty acid-derived lipids supporting vegetative growth
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports vegetative growth redundantly with OsENR1
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastid type II fatty acid synthase pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: same ENR reduction step in elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ENR paralog inferred to catalyze the final reduction step of plastid fatty acid elongation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supports precursor supply for general growth rather than male fertility alone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established for male fertility; redundant for vegetative growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not required alone for male fertility, but redundantly essential with OsENR1 for normal seedling growth and survival.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsENR2 is highly expressed in shoots and leaves. 2. Single CRISPR mutants are fertile and phenotypically normal. 3. OsENR1/OsENR2 double mutants show curly leaves, dwarfism, and gradual death, indicating redundant essential function in vegetative development.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High transcript levels in shoots and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; CRISPR/Cas9 single and double knockout analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined disruption with OsENR1 causes lethality-like seedling defects.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core in the evolutionary/function section, but not a primary male-fertility determinant alone.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not ideal as a single fertility-control target; important caution for stacked editing of ENR paralogs.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Redundant support of vegetative development; not individually essential for male fertility
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastid type II fatty acid synthase pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: same ENR reduction step in elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ENR paralog inferred to catalyze the final reduction step of plastid fatty acid elongation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supports precursor supply, partially redundant with other ENRs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; not individually essential for male fertility
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single knockout has normal vegetative growth and male fertility; may partially redundantly buffer anther lipid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmENR2 transcript is detected in anthers at S8b and S9. 2. ZmENR2 localizes to plastids. 3. CRISPR single mutants show normal vegetative growth and male fertility, indicating nonessential or redundant role alone.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in anthers at S8b and S9
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can contribute redundantly in combination with other ENRs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as possible partial redundancy with ZmENR1 in anther cuticle formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited value alone; relevant when engineering multi-gene ENR modules.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fatty acid-derived lipids supporting vegetative growth
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for vegetative growth and viability
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastid type II fatty acid synthase pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: same ENR reduction step in elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ENR paralog inferred to catalyze the final reduction step of plastid fatty acid elongation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supports general fatty acid precursor supply for vegetative development
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential for vegetative function but not established for anther fertility alone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal seedling development; knockout causes curly leaves, dwarfism, and eventual death.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmENR3 is mainly expressed in stem, leaf, cob, and silk. 2. ZmENR3 localizes to plastids. 3. Single zmenr3 mutants and triple mutants display severe seedling defects and die, indicating indispensable vegetative function.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly detected in stem, leaf, cob, and silk
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; subcellular localization; CRISPR/Cas9 single and triple knockout phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Vegetative ENR paralog functionally diverged from reproductive ZmENR1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid disrupting in breeding/editing strategies targeting male sterility.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: anther cuticle lipids and pollen exine precursors
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: anther epidermal cuticle and pollen exine
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct positive regulator within the ZmMS1-driven feedback repression loop that activates plastid lipid-biosynthetic genes ZmENR1 and ZmHAD1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lipid precursor biosynthesis for anther cuticle and pollen exine formation by activating ZmENR1 and ZmHAD1 transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts within the ZmMS1-driven feedback repression loop
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ZmMS1-driven feedback repression loop context during anther development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression overlaps with ZmENR1 and ZmHAD1 from S6 to S10
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR in myb84 mutant; transient dual-luciferase reporter assay; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB84 expression overlaps with ZmENR1/ZmHAD1 during anther development. 2. Loss of ZmMYB84 reduces ZmENR1 and ZmHAD1 transcript abundance. 3. ZmMYB84 directly binds and activates both ZmENR1 and ZmHAD1 promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Anther RNA-seq referenced; ChIP-seq dataset GSE220176 used for related regulatory context
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream regulator to coordinately enhance plastid lipid precursor supply for male fertility traits.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: anther cuticle lipids and pollen exine precursors
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: anther epidermal cuticle and pollen exine
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central negative regulator driving the feedback repression loop that directly represses ZmENR1 and ZmHAD1 to fine-tune lipid biosynthesis and exine/cuticle formation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses ZmENR1 and ZmHAD1 transcription to fine-tune plastid fatty acid biosynthesis during anther development.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Driver of a feedback repression loop (ZmbHLH51-ZmMYB84-ZmMS7-ZmMS1)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression overlaps with ZmENR1 and ZmHAD1 from S6 to S10
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR in ms1-alb mutant; transient dual-luciferase reporter assay; EMSA; ChIP-seq
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmENR1 and ZmHAD1 are upregulated in ms1-alb anthers. 2. ZmMS1 suppresses ZmENR1 and ZmHAD1 promoter activity in protoplast assays. 3. EMSA and ChIP-seq support direct binding of ZmMS1 to both promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulator for tuning anther lipid metabolism and male fertility; manipulation requires caution because it controls a broader fertility network.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously known as ZmLBD30; here specifically shown to regulate plastid de novo fatty acid genes in addition to ER lipid-metabolic genes.
